title: A Decent Proposal

jokes.text: Remind me to never get shot again.
jokes.setup: Character complaining about a difficult week
jokes.punchline: Casually referring to getting shot as something to avoid next time
jokes.explanation: Strong dark humor with perfect economy. Will's casual tone about being shot creates excellent contrast.

jokes.text: It's always hard the first time. I remember mine.
jokes.setup: Will says getting shot was hard
jokes.punchline: Jazz treats getting shot like a normal life milestone everyone experiences
jokes.explanation: Jazz's matter-of-fact delivery about being shot before is darkly funny and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I'd forgotten how much Jewel hates anchovies on her pizza.
jokes.setup: Jazz remembering his first time getting shot
jokes.punchline: The memory is about pizza toppings, not the shooting itself
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up expectation about trauma but delivers mundane detail. Very Jazz-like absurdity.

jokes.text: Nurse Sigmund has on the exact same outfit.
jokes.setup: Hilary telling nurse to hurry and change
jokes.punchline: Her concern is about fashion coordination, not medical urgency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - she's superficial enough to care about nurses wearing the same outfit in a hospital.

jokes.text: I'm sure my arm won't feel a thing.
jokes.setup: Will saying he's on a natural high from leaving hospital
jokes.punchline: Suggesting his arm is numb from drugs, not natural happiness
jokes.explanation: Decent setup for the injection gag that follows. Will's self-aware humor about being medicated.

jokes.text: [SCREAMS]
jokes.setup: Will confidently saying his arm won't feel the injection
jokes.punchline: He screams in pain immediately after
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy payoff. Predictable but effective execution of the setup.

jokes.text: Check it out, Will. I'm the black dude on ER.
jokes.setup: Jazz wearing hospital scrubs
jokes.punchline: His excitement about resembling the token Black character on a medical TV show
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Jazz - his naive excitement and casual reference to tokenism in 90s TV.

jokes.text: It doesn't have to be a toy to be fun. Check this out. Clear. [SCREAMING THEN CRASH]
jokes.setup: Jazz playing with medical equipment despite Will's warning
jokes.punchline: He uses defibrillator paddles and gets shocked/crashes
jokes.explanation: Pure Jazz behavior - ignoring obvious danger for fun. Physical comedy escalates appropriately.

jokes.text: Oh, cool, because I never could before.
jokes.setup: Physical therapist asking if he'll be able to slam-dunk again
jokes.punchline: Will reveals he never could slam-dunk in the first place
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect timing. Subverts expectation about athletic recovery.

jokes.text: Yeah, I fell, unh... in love with you, baby.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if Will fell
jokes.punchline: Will turns literal fall into romantic declaration
jokes.explanation: Smooth recovery line typical of Will's character. Corny but charming wordplay.

jokes.text: Will, did you take another one of those yellow pills?
jokes.setup: Will proposing marriage out of nowhere
jokes.punchline: Lisa's assumption that he's high on medication
jokes.explanation: Lisa's practical reaction to Will's sudden proposal is both funny and realistic.

jokes.text: I can give you this one around my collar.
jokes.setup: Will apologizing for not having an engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Offers to give her his hospital bracelet/collar
jokes.explanation: Sweet but absurd gesture. Will making the best of his hospital situation.

jokes.text: WILL & LISA: Don't touch that!
jokes.setup: Hilary reaching for something in the hospital room
jokes.punchline: Both Will and Lisa urgently stop her from touching medical equipment
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jazz's earlier accident. Establishes Hilary as equally dangerous around equipment.

jokes.text: Oh, it's not their fault. They're setting up for your surprise party. Well, now that you dragged it out of me.
jokes.setup: Will disappointed that family isn't there for his big day
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately ruins surprise party then blames Will for 'dragging it out'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - she can't keep a secret but deflects blame. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: There has got to be an easier way to call the nurse.
jokes.setup: Hilary just accidentally triggered some kind of emergency alarm
jokes.punchline: Her casual complaint about the difficulty of summoning help
jokes.explanation: Hilary's obliviousness to causing chaos while complaining about inconvenience is very in-character.

jokes.text: And I don't think what you're doing is good for anybody.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil commenting on someone's activity being unhealthy
jokes.punchline: Ashley's comeback about Phil's own unhealthy habits
jokes.explanation: Good sibling-like comeback. Ashley holding her own against Uncle Phil's criticism.

jokes.text: Oh, I love you too, Will.
jokes.setup: Will describing someone special as 'warm, caring, and extremely sensitive'
jokes.punchline: Carlton assumes Will is talking about him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his earnest assumption that Will's romantic speech is about him. Great character-driven misdirection.

jokes.text: Go sit down.
jokes.setup: Carlton interrupting Will's romantic announcement
jokes.punchline: Will's curt dismissal of Carlton's misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Will's exasperation with Carlton is economically expressed.

jokes.text: You know, this precludes any possibility of us getting together. To do what?
jokes.setup: Ashley saying Will's engagement eliminates their romantic possibility
jokes.punchline: Will's confused 'To do what?' reveals he never considered it
jokes.explanation: Excellent awkward humor. Ashley's teenage crush meets Will's genuine confusion. Great timing and character work.

jokes.text: Her ex-cellmate broke out. They're having dinner.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking where Jazz's girlfriend Jewel is
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that Jewel is having dinner with an escaped convict
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Jazz's characteristic casualness. Reveals both Jewel's criminal past and Jazz's acceptance of chaos.

jokes.text: I mean, come on, I'm still decorating my new house. When would I have time to buy you a gift?
jokes.setup: Hilary agreeing that Will's marriage is too soon
jokes.punchline: Her concern is entirely about gift-buying logistics, not Will's wellbeing
jokes.explanation: Pure Hilary selfishness disguised as family concern. Perfectly captures her character priorities.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, come here. My foot wants to congratulate your butt.
jokes.setup: Will asking if anyone will congratulate him
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv threatens physical punishment disguised as congratulations
jokes.explanation: Classic parental threat with creative phrasing. Aunt Viv's protective anger expressed humorously.

jokes.text: Well, now, just what exactly is 15 percent of a frozen burrito?
jokes.setup: Will mentioning he gets tips at his college food service job
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's sarcastic calculation of tips on cheap food
jokes.explanation: Uncle Phil's lawyer-like precision applied to demolishing Will's financial prospects. Good escalation in the financial reality check.

jokes.text: Hey, I watch The Cosby Show too, Uncle Phil. This is that episode where Theo drops out of school, right? Yeah, well, a couple more Pudding Pops, you'll be there.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil giving Will a reality check about finances and education
jokes.punchline: Will recognizing the Cosby Show parallel and mocking Uncle Phil's weight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on sitcom tropes while delivering a classic fat joke. Will's street smarts recognize the lecture format.

jokes.text: What's love got to do with it? GEOFFREY [SINGING]: Got to do with it, got to do with it
jokes.setup: Will saying he and Lisa will be fine because they have love
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv quotes Tina Turner song, Geoffrey automatically provides backup vocals
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Geoffrey and musical timing. Perfect butler-like assistance extending to backup vocals.

jokes.text: [IN HIGH-PITCHED VOICE] I'm okay. I'm okay.
jokes.setup: Jazz getting hurt in tug-of-war over cake
jokes.punchline: High-pitched voice indicating injury despite claiming to be fine
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy payoff - voice change indicates pain

jokes.text: My goodness, those doctors must have crossed some wires in there.
jokes.setup: Will's reflexes are backwards - hits himself when doctor tries to test knee
jokes.punchline: Therapist's dry observation about surgical mistakes
jokes.explanation: Good medical professional humor. Dry delivery about serious surgical error is appropriately understated.

jokes.text: Don't wanna get what? A ticket. I'm double-parked outside.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to tell Will she doesn't want to get married
jokes.punchline: She chickens out and makes up excuse about parking
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Lisa's inability to hurt Will leads to awkward misdirection about parking tickets.

jokes.text: See? It wasn't that hard, was it?
jokes.setup: Lisa finally admits she doesn't want to marry Will
jokes.punchline: Will's bizarrely upbeat response to being rejected
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment. Will's shock manifests as inappropriate positivity. Great reaction to devastating news.

jokes.text: See? It wasn't that hard, was it?
jokes.setup: Lisa finally saying she doesn't want to marry him
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic encouragement while clearly devastated
jokes.explanation: Painful irony that shows Will's defense mechanism - using humor to mask devastation

jokes.text: D-U-M-P-E-D. 'Dumped.' And you also made 'lonely,' 'shocked' and 'pathetic.'
jokes.setup: Will playing Scrabble while depressed about Lisa
jokes.punchline: His Scrabble tiles all spell words describing his emotional state
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag. Using Scrabble as emotional expression is creative and perfectly executed. Great way to show Will's state of mind.

jokes.text: Next on Montel Williams... Women Who Won't Marry and the Losers Who Ask Them.
jokes.setup: Will channel surfing while dealing with rejection
jokes.punchline: TV show topic directly mirrors his situation
jokes.explanation: Good comedic timing. Universe seeming to mock Will's situation through random TV programming.

jokes.text: Just make mine a single-sized serving.
jokes.setup: Will asking for the same meal as Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Requesting normal portion size, implying Phil's portions are enormous
jokes.explanation: Subtle fat joke about Uncle Phil. Will's depression manifest in even simple food orders.

jokes.text: My God, man, I'm in a wheelchair.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil telling Will to butter his own biscuit
jokes.punchline: Will's dramatic appeal to his disability for sympathy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will milking his condition. Uncle Phil's tough love meets Will's manipulation.

jokes.text: Sir, when you're finished eating, I've pre-pasted your toothbrush upstairs.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil lecturing Will about being self-sufficient
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey immediately offers to help Phil with basic hygiene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment exposing Uncle Phil's hypocrisy. Excellent timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: Which reminds me. Lisa called.
jokes.setup: Will saying he never wants to hear Lisa's name again
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey immediately mentions she called
jokes.explanation: Classic comedy timing. Perfect setup and immediate contradiction for maximum awkwardness.

jokes.text: You are so accident-prone.
jokes.setup: Will refusing to go anywhere with Carlton because he gets hurt
jokes.punchline: Carlton blaming Will for being accident-prone when Carlton causes the accidents
jokes.explanation: Good callback to running gag. Carlton's complete lack of self-awareness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Every time I get around you, I wind up getting hurt. You are so accident-prone.
jokes.setup: Will refusing to go to therapy with Carlton
jokes.punchline: Blaming Carlton for being accident-prone when Will is the one always getting hurt
jokes.explanation: Good callback to running gag about Carlton's clumsiness affecting Will

jokes.text: Being in a wheelchair is no excuse... for wearing the same shirt two days in a row.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying she's disappointed in Will, setting up for emotional support
jokes.punchline: Her concern is entirely about fashion, not his emotional state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment. Subverts expectation of emotional support for pure vanity concern. Excellent character writing.

jokes.text: You would think hitting the concrete like that would've made more of an impact.
jokes.setup: Discussing Hilary's fiancé's bungee jumping death
jokes.punchline: Hilary's unintentional pun about 'impact' regarding fatal accident
jokes.explanation: Dark humor disguised as Hilary's obliviousness. The pun on 'impact' works on multiple levels - physical and emotional.

jokes.text: Poor, poor... Tip of the tongue, Will. Please help me out. Trevor.
jokes.setup: Hilary getting emotional about her dead fiancé
jokes.punchline: She can't remember his name mid-emotional speech
jokes.explanation: Brutal character moment. Hilary's shallow nature revealed even in grief. Perfectly uncomfortable and funny.

jokes.text: Well, stupid is as stupid does. Well, what is that supposed to mean? I don't know, but the movie made like a half a billion dollars.
jokes.setup: Lisa calling Will stupid, Will responding with Forrest Gump quote
jokes.punchline: Will admits he doesn't know what it means but cites the movie's success
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with self-aware twist. Will's logic about movie success is characteristically street-smart but shallow.

jokes.text: I don't know, but the movie made like a half a billion dollars.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking what his Forrest Gump quote means
jokes.punchline: Will admits he doesn't know but cites the movie's success as justification
jokes.explanation: Good recovery that turns weak reference into commentary on pop culture success

jokes.text: Baby, I don't wanna have to shoot you every six months just to hold on to you.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining her fears about Will's proposal timing
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she'd have to repeatedly shoot Will to keep him interested
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor line. Takes the 'getting shot made him vulnerable' premise to absurd logical conclusion. Very quotable.

jokes.text: You wouldn't really bust a cap at me, though, would you? Not unless I have to.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Lisa would actually shoot him
jokes.punchline: Lisa's ominous 'not unless I have to' response
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the shooting joke. Lisa's mock-threatening response maintains the dark humor thread.

jokes.text: Tyler and I were saving it for our honeymoon. Trevor, Hilary. Right, Trevor, whatever.
jokes.setup: Hilary offering champagne she was saving with her fiancé
jokes.punchline: Gets his name wrong again, gets corrected, dismisses it with 'whatever'
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of the Trevor name gag. Hilary's 'whatever' attitude toward her dead fiancé's name is perfectly callous.

jokes.text: [WILL YELLS] [SPLASH] [ALL SCREAM]
jokes.setup: Will saying accidents happen while trying to be gracious
jokes.punchline: Immediately has another accident, falling into something with water
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy ending. Predictable but well-executed callback to Will's accident-prone nature around family.

tmdb_overview: Mr. and Mrs. Smith? Will pops the question from his hospital room. And Lisa's answer is...
